title: DebuggerDisplayAttribute strings should reference existing members
description: "DebuggerDisplayAttribute" strings should reference existing membersAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?TheDebuggerDisplayAttributeis used to determine how an object is displayed in the debugger window.TheDebuggerDisplayAttributeconstructor takes a single mandatory argument: the string to be displayed in the value column for instances of the type. Any text within curly braces is evaluated as the name of a field or property, or any complex expression containing method calls and operators.Naming a non-existent member between curly braces will result in a CS0103 error in the debug window when debugging objects. Although there is no impact on the production code, providing a wrong value can lead to difficulties when debugging the application.Thi
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect DebuggerDisplay attributes with member references that don't exist
logic.triggering_conditions: DebuggerDisplay attribute references a non-existent member
logic.exclusion_conditions: References to existing members, ToString, GetHashCode, etc.
never_trigger_on: References to base class methods like ToString
logic.reasoning: DebuggerDisplay attributes with invalid member references cause CS0103 errors in the debugger. This custom function extracts the DebuggerDisplay string, parses member references in curly braces, extracts actual class members (properties, fields, methods), and validates that all references exist.
